classification.analysis: Starmer faces internal revolt after catastrophic May 7 local elections saw Labour lose 1,500 councillors while Reform UK won 27% nationally. Over 80 MPs demand his resignation, four ministers quit, and Andy Burnham prepares leadership challenge via upcoming by-election. Reform leads polls at 28% versus Labour's 19%.

Merz faces record-low 15% approval rating after criticizing Trump over Iran war, triggering US troop withdrawal announcement and auto tariffs. AfD now leads polls at 27% versus coalition's 37%, eroding the CDU/SPD parliamentary majority to unsustainable levels as state elections loom.

Macron at historic 18% approval hosts Africa summit in Kenya announcing €23bn investment. Far-right Bardella leads 2027 polls at 37%, Macron's party at 10%. Political deadlock persists after snap election backfired. RN positioned for power.

Sánchez faces sustained pressure as PSOE-Sumar polls 7 points behind PP, with Vox consolidating near 18%. His defiance of Trump over Iran war bases and wife's April corruption charges dominate headlines. Regional PSOE losses in Aragon and Extremadura signal vulnerability, though anti-war stance remains popular domestically.

Frederiksen remains caretaker after March election delivered her party's worst result since 1903. Coalition talks stalled when Moderates leader blocked left-leaning option on April 10, demanding five-party centrist arrangement. Danish People's Party has surged to 11.4% and now leads blue bloc, with leader calling for Muslim removal as condition for support.

One Nation surged to 32% in post-budget polling, overtaking Labor at 30.5%, with the far-right now leading primaries. Albanese faces 59% disapproval amid intense criticism over broken tax promises on negative gearing and capital gains, with journalists accusing him of lying to voters. Labor's 51-49 two-party lead over One Nation is described as 'too close to call.'

Martin faces by-election drubbing and internal leadership pressure after former Taoiseach Bertie Ahern's immigration remarks damaged Fianna Fáil's campaign. Coalition polling has fallen to 33.7%, losing its majority projection, while Independent Ireland and Aontú surge to combined 16% on rural discontent. Leadership challenge expected post-EU Presidency.

Jetten's minority coalition polls at just 36.7% three months after taking office, already below the parliamentary majority threshold. While the PVV has collapsed from internal splits, the combined far-right remains strong at roughly 21%. Modi's visit this week underscored Jetten's pro-European reset, but domestic polling suggests the coalition's parliamentary arithmetic is already failing.

Montenegro reinforced police in Lisbon and Porto on May 12 amid security concerns, but faces mounting pressure from the PS opposition, which warns 'patience has its limits' after five unanswered dialogue attempts. His minority coalition trails both Socialists and Chega in polls, governing through a precarious balancing act that has seen immigration restrictions pass with far-right support.

Støre's Labour minority government faces the Progress Party leading polls at 29% after doubling its vote share in September 2025. Despite winning re-election eight months ago, the far-right now consistently leads nationally, positioning itself as the government-in-waiting for 2029.

Takaichi won a historic February landslide with 316 LDP seats but faces approval erosion from 79% to 56-70% amid local election losses and smear scandal allegations. Far-right Sanseito grew from 2 to 15 seats but remains marginal at 3%. LDP party approval at 41%.

Michal's Reform-E200 coalition polls at catastrophic 14%, down from already-low February levels. The far-right EKRE remains at 12.6%, contained below 15% and trailing conservative Isamaa (26%) and Centre (22%). Recently proposed EU customs duties on Russian goods at Cyprus summit. Expelled Social Democrats in March 2025, shrinking coalition. Certain 2027 defeat looms.

Kristersson's Moderate-led coalition governs with Sweden Democrats' external support through the Tidö Agreement. In April 2026, he signaled openness to full SD coalition post-election. May polls show opposition ahead by 10.7 points, with male Tidö support dropping four points. Far-right already wields policy power while coalition trails badly.

Orpo's coalition with the nationalist Finns Party has collapsed to just 36% support after implementing harsh austerity amid 9.5% unemployment. Multiple racism scandals involving Finns Party ministers have been weathered without accountability. The opposition SDP now leads by 6+ points. Recent drone security alerts added pressure.

Mitsotakis unveiled constitutional reforms while warning of Iran conflict's economic impact. Despite the OPEKEPE farm-subsidy scandal and polling decline from 40% to 30%, ND maintains a commanding lead over fragmented opposition. Greek Solution far-right polls at only 8.6%, well-contained below threshold.

Stocker's three-party coalition clings to a knife-edge 49.7% majority while FPÖ surges to 36% in polls. Herbert Kickl declared 2026 'our year' at massive May Day rally, promising power is 'one election away.' Coalition dubbed 'Verliererampel' (loser coalition) amid economic contraction and eroding public support.

Belgium's Constitutional Court suspended De Wever's strict asylum rules in May 2026, undermining his signature migration policy. Vlaams Belang leads Flanders polling at 27.3% versus N-VA's 20.7%, though the Arizona coalition retains 57% national support. Major union protests target pension cuts and unemployment benefit time-limits. The cordon sanitaire holds but faces sustained far-right pressure.

Luxon faces coalition crisis as NZ First surges to 12-14% and Winston Peters rivals him in preferred PM polls. Late April saw emergency talks after Peters released emails showing foreign policy disputes over Iran war support. Coalition projected to narrowly retain power despite National's collapse to 28-30%.

Babiš governs with far-right SPD in coalition since December 2025. Recent tensions include coalition partner Motorists' inflammatory rhetoric toward civil servants and ongoing power struggle with President Pavel over NATO summit representation and Ukraine funding commitments. Coalition polling slightly down but maintains parliamentary majority at 53% of seats.

Trump's approval cratered to 34-37% amid Iran war stalemate and failed China summit. Independents' net approval plunged to -38, down 18 points yearly. China visit yielded no breakthroughs on Iran peace or trade while Xi lectured on Taiwan. Midterm headwinds intensifying with underwater approval in all seven battleground Senate states.

Magyar's Tisza party holds a commanding 67% polling lead one week after defeating Orbán. He recently demanded President Sulyok and Orbán-era officials resign by May 31, a controversial power move that has broad public support but risks overreach. The far-right Mi Hazánk remains marginal at 6%. The honeymoon is intact but governing Hungary's damaged institutions will test him.

Fico's May 2026 Moscow visit for Victory Day—defying EU consensus—epitomizes his government's authoritarian drift. With the coalition polling at just 28.7% and Progressive Slovakia leading, mass protests continue against criminal code reforms that benefit allies. The dismantling of anti-corruption institutions, attacks on NGOs, and pro-Russian foreign policy align with Orbán-style democratic backsliding. German Chancellor Merz's cancellation of his Slovakia visit signals growing EU isolation for Bratislava's government.

Milei's approval collapsed to 35% amid mass May 12 university protests and corruption scandals involving Cabinet chief Adorni's lavish spending. Wages have fallen 9% since he took office while his labor law overhaul and university funding cuts sparked nationwide demonstrations. Opposition remains fragmented despite mounting social discontent over his austerity-driven institutional restructuring.

Netanyahu's coalition faces imminent collapse after ultra-Orthodox faction Degel HaTorah called for Knesset dissolution on May 12 over failure to exempt Haredim from military service. Elections may come in September instead of October. His Likud base is fracturing badly with forty-two percent of 2022 voters defecting or considering it, while far-right Otzma Yehudit maintains leverage as indispensable coalition partner.

Erdoğan consolidates authoritarian control through judiciary and security apparatus. In May 2026, AKP drafted legislation expanding presidential powers over maritime zones. Government arrested hundreds of opposition representatives after 2024 municipal defeats. İmamoğlu remains jailed on charges critics call politically motivated. Economic stress persists with 58% inflation despite relatively stable approval.

Tusk's coalition has lost its projected parliamentary majority as combined far-right forces (Confederation plus KKP) surge to 24.3%, nearly matching PiS. After PiS-backed Nawrocki won the 2025 presidency, far-right momentum has accelerated with coalition partner Poland 2050 fracturing internally. Recent tensions over US troop redeployment saw opposition accuse Tusk of prioritizing Germany over Polish security, while a potential right-wing majority looms for 2027.

Carney's approval has slipped from the 60s to 58% following his Davos speech, where he outlined a middle-powers strategy. Despite the decline, Liberals maintain a commanding 11-15 point lead. Recent focus includes forging closer European ties and striking an Alberta export diversification deal. The PPC remains negligible at 1-2%, posing no far-right threat.

Lee maintains strong 60% approval nearly a year after defeating the far-right following Yoon's martial law crisis. Recent diplomatic success hosting US-China talks showcases pragmatic governance, though approval has slipped from 71% peak amid welfare spending concerns and cost-of-living pressures among centrist voters.

Past week dominated by sovereignty tensions with Trump over alleged CIA operations in Mexico and U.S. indictments of Morena officials. Sheinbaum denied CIA presence while maintaining cordial dialogue with Trump. Approval declining amid corruption and crime concerns, but no organized far-right opposition exists—traditional parties PAN/PRI remain weakened and fragmented.
